_comment: Digital Insurgency 2E voice casting. voice_id values are placeholders — fill with real ElevenLabs voice IDs at the recording gate. Suggested voices are directional, not final. REAPER intentionally has NO voice (read as flat machine notifications by NARRATOR).

NARRATOR.description: The Broadcast voice + Space Pirate Zero. Raw, punk, confessional; Atlanta cadence; warm at the worst table.
NARRATOR.why: Single voice carries both the cyberpunk broadcast narration and the SPZ closer — the show's spine.

ZERO.description: Zara Okonkwo-Reyes, 31, she/her. Direct, technical, economical — boxing economy. Dry, never cruel.
ZERO.why: Protagonist. Quotes math at people; cuts her own pitch when she misreads a room.
ZERO.voice_name: Sarah - Mature, Reassuring, Confident (young, american, professional)

GHOST.description: The Curator, it/its. Clinical, alien, precise. Consider a lightly processed / synthetic treatment.
GHOST.why: Non-human AI ally. Aphorisms about selection vs. creation; states, never predicts.

BISHOP.description: Marcus Bishop, 47, he/him. Measured, strategic, calm — the calmest person in any room. Speaks in chess and policy.
BISHOP.why: The inside man. Deliberate stillness; minimal words.

SARAH.description: Sarah Chen, VP Compliance, late 40s. Exhausted, practical, no patience for vision.
SARAH.why: The bleeding-neck user buyer; emotional anchor of the wedge.

KEISHA.description: Keisha Williams, 24, flashback only. Plain, stumbling, young. No epigrams.
KEISHA.why: The ghost in Bishop's memory; must not sound like ZERO.

COUNCIL.description: PRISM Architecture Board. One flat, institutional collective voice. Not sinister — corrective.
COUNCIL.why: Abstract antagonist. 'Authenticity is a market inefficiency. We correct inefficiencies.'

BOSS.description: Utility slot for per-episode boss archetypes (the 'We'll Get to It' VP, Sunk-Cost Guardian, Growth Hacker, CFO Richard Park, CISO, Iron Mike, Dr. Vance, the Innovation Lead...). Cast distinct per episode.
BOSS.why: One rotating antagonist-of-the-week role; keep each distinct within its own episode.

_REAPER_NOTE: REAPER has NO voice_id by design. Its 'lines' are system notifications / automated emails read FLATLY by NARRATOR. Never cast it as a character.
